title: Team USA: Ryôma Echizen
overview: After Ryoma is forced to withdraw from the Japan U-17 training camp, Ryoga invites him to play for the US team.

title: Pre-World Cup Kicks Off!
overview: Teams from all over the world competing in the U-17 World Cup gather to start things off with the Pre-World Cup exhibition matches.

title: A Moment Ahead in the Future
overview: Pre-World Cup (World Cup), 3rd match. In the champion Germany, captain Bork finally goes to the court. The challenge is the Tokugawa & Yukimura pair. However, shortly after the start of the game, Yukimura was "deprived of his five senses." Tokugawa will fight alone against the German pair while hiding Yukimura. Meanwhile, Yukimura was also fighting loneliness. Yukimura's ears, deprived of his five senses and fighting "fear alone" in the closed darkness, heard his voice overcoming it while being deprived of his five senses.

title: Prince vs Prince
overview: Japan's Pre-World Cup match ended. After seeing the match and leaving the venue, Ryoma encounters Sakurano, who is entwined by local bad guys. Doberman attacks Ryoma, who confronts the bad guys while sheltering Sakurano. It was a boy who straddled Hakuba who rejected it. He shoots the ball with a tennis racket while straddling a horse and drives away the bad guys. Ryoma, who was mistaken for a boyfriend by a boy who was fascinated by Sakurano, was challenged to play a game, and they would fight each other in tennis while riding horses.

title: Upset
overview: Finally, the World Cup main draw will begin. Japan's first opponent is the Greek national team. If they manage to beat them, their next round opponent would be the Australia who has managed to beat, Switzerland, who was ranked 2nd in the world.

title: Absolute Away Game
overview: In the Japan team's first doubles match against Australia, Sanada's tricky shots initially baffle his opponents, but they quickly adapt their defense.

title: Chess King
overview: The Japan team plays with renewed vigor following Atobe’s cunning ruse. Finally figuring out Milman’s Tachyon serve, Fuji moves to break the deadlock.

title: One's Resolve... One's Conditions
overview: Discouraged by two straight doubles losses, the Japan team heads into the singles matches. Akutsu goes up against the menacing Swiss captain, Amadeus.

title: Feelings for My Friends
overview: After seeing Akutsu's match against Switzerland, Ryoma seems to have developed some feelings of doubt within him...

title: Beyond the Bible
overview: Ryoma finally returns to the Japan national team. With Ryoma as a member, the opponent in the quarterfinals will be France, ranked 3rd in the world. Shiraishi, who was selected as a member, was worried about whether his "honor student" tennis would be able to compete against the world.

title: The Worst Doubles Pair
overview: After Yanagi rebukes Mori for his past attitude toward training, the two must overcome their differences if they want to beat their French opponents.

title: Prince of Ace
overview: Ryoma's match with Chardard continues in three singles matches of Japan versus France; the victorious team will advance to play Germany.

title: Samurai vs Knight
overview: Ryoma's match with Chardard continues.

title: The Mightiest Germany: The Night Before the Match!
overview: The Japanese team is resolutely preparing for the match against Germany, aiming to advance to the finals afterward. Captain Byodoin trusts his players, who do not give up despite this challenge.

title: Bluebird
overview: In the first match of the U-17 World Cup Semifinals it's Team Japan's Oni versus Team Germany's Q･P. Can Oni hold his ground against Team Germany's No. 2 player?

title: For Whom Do You Grow Strong
overview: The first match of the World Cup Semifinals continues between Oni and Q･P. Oni shines with a mysterious light that resembles the Pinnacle of Perfection and becomes seemingly invincible.

title: The Final Illusion
overview: In the next match, Team Japan's Duke and Nio face off against Schneider and Belti, two of the three tennis pros on Team Germany.

title: Tezuka vs. Yukimura
overview: Continuing the World Cup Semifinals is Team Germany’s Tezuka versus Team Japan’s Yukimura in #2 singles. Tezuka is now their opponent, so Team Japan goes all out to gain victory against their former teammate.

title: Zero Senses Tennis
overview: Yukimura prepares to counter Tezuka's Pinnacle of Perfection in order to prove to Akaya, his team, and himself that you don't have to give up on tennis even if you can't use the Pinnacle of Perfection.

title: The Future Beyond the Fierce Match
overview: Every time Yukimura takes away Tezuka's five senses, Tezuka regaains them using the Pinnacle of Perfection. Can Yukimura be able to turn the tables or will Tezuka hold on to his lead?

title: Team Spain, Ryoga Echizen
overview: In the other semifinals bracket, Team USA is competing with Team Spain for a spot at the finals. Ryoga Echizen, who is now playing for Team Spain, clashes with Ralph Reinhart of Team USA.

title: Focus Burst vs. Pinnacle of Perfection
overview: Kirihara is determined to beat Siegfried's Pinnacle of Perfection with his Focus Burst. The two battle it out as their doubles partners watch on.

title: The Outcome of the Tiebreak
overview: The second set of the doubles match between Tanegashima-Kirihara and Bismarck-Siegfried gets drawn into a tiebreak. If Team Japan loses this set, they will be eliminated from the semifinals.

title: Final Set
overview: The doubles pairs Tanegashima-Kirihara and Bismarck-Siegfried ramp it up for this final set, showing teamwork that activates skills never seen before.

title: Spiral Baptism
overview: In the last match of the semifinals, Byodoin and Volk go head-to-head in a mighty battle to bring their teams through to the finals.

title: Life-or-Death Tennis
overview: Byodoin puts his life on the line against Germany's Volk in an intense epilogue to the World Cup semifinals.
